subject: Re: AREtomo to WARP
body:
Hi all,

thank you for your responses.

The AREtomo command I used is:
(ARETOMO_executable_path) -InMrc TS_01.mrc -OutMrc 
TS_01_bin8_correcttiltangle.mrc -VolZ 1640 -OutBin 8 -AngFile 
TS_01_tiltangle.txt -Wbp 1 -Patch 4 4 -OutImod 2 -TiltCor -1

The binning in AREtomo is 8. When I reimport into WARP, for the "IMPORT 
TILT SERIES FROM IMOD" window I enter 17.464 for pixel size (binned 8X) and 
the dose is 4.4 per tilt (180 total dose, 41 tilts). Everything imports 
correctly. For AREtomo I used the Wbp 1 flag, so it's WBP I believe. I am 
not sure what WARP uses. 

Not sure what you mean if I use VolZ 0... if the volume of Z for AREtomo is 
zero then there will be no tomogram generated. I'll try that.

Jesse



On Wednesday, June 14, 2023 at 5:34:34 PM UTC+2 lorenzo...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi Jesse,
>
> As mentioned by Tom, the only file needed (and read) by Warp is the `xf` 
> file. This gets combined with the angles from the mdoc file to get a full 
> set of transformations for tilt series alignment.
>
> Some ideas:
> - did you provide `-TiltCor` in AreTomo? If so, you need to update the 
> mdoc file to match the new angles. Otherwise, disable it entirely (-1) or 
> set it to 0.
> - did you provide all the reconstruction-related options (dose, pixel 
> size, etc) and are you sure the units are correct?
> - did you use Sart (default) or WBP for reconstruction? Sart looks much 
> more contrasty to the naked eye; try -Wbp to check whether that is the 
> difference you see.
> - does the aligned tilt series look well aligned if you use -VolZ 0?
>
> Good luck!
> Lorenzo
>
> On Wed, Jun 14, 2023 at 5:30 PM Pranav Shah <Prana...@strubi.ox.ac.uk> 
> wrote:
>
>> If you corrected for pre-tilt in aretomo youbwill get poor results.
>>
>> On Wed, 14 Jun 2023 at 16:03, tom dendooven <tom.de...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Jesse, 
>>>
>>> Are you looking at deconvolved tomograms from warp or unfiltered ones? 
>>> Or is the binning significantly different between the Warp and aretomo 
>>> tomograms? 
>>>
>>> Warp doesn't need a TAsolution.log file. 
>>>
>>> Best wishes,
>>> Tom
>>>
>>> On Wed, 14 Jun 2023 at 15:31, Jesse Hansen <bluu...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>
>>>> hi all,
>>>>
>>>> I started in WARP for movie alignment then exported the stack "to 
>>>> IMOD".   However I used AREtomo to process instead of Etomo, using the flag 
>>>> -OUTImod 2 in order to get the xf file for importing into WARP.  The 
>>>> AREtomo reconstruction looks fantastic.   Now I reimport into WARP without 
>>>> issue, estimate the CTF, check handedness, then reconstruct tomograms.    
>>>> It all succeeds, but the output tomograms are really poor quality and I 
>>>> can't figure out why.   They certainly look similar to AREtomo in that I 
>>>> can see some proteins where they should be, but the contrast is really 
>>>> poor.  
>>>>
>>>> Inverting contrast in tomo reconstruction didn't help.   The FFT looks 
>>>> nice and has resolution to 6A, clear Thon rings which match well with the 
>>>> calculated CTF.  AREtomo did not output a TAsolutions.log file that I Can 
>>>> find, which I thought WARP needs.  However WARP did seem to successfully 
>>>> import the data using just the XF file.   I tried clicking "dont invert 
>>>> tilt angles" during import because perhaps AREtomo is different from IMOD 
>>>> (explained in WARP tutorial) but that had no effect.  Hard to tell which 
>>>> was better.  I tried reprocessing AREtomo without the flag -FlipVol hoping 
>>>> that might fix an issue with the coordinate system but it also had no 
>>>> effect.  Overall it seems like the protein is definitely visible in the 
>>>> final reconstruction but it just looks really poor contrast.  Maybe 
>>>> something is going wrong with the CTF correction or...  maybe it's just 
>>>> that the WARP reconstruction looks WAY worse than the AREtomo 
>>>> reconstruction.   
>>>>
>>>> Any help appreciated, thanks.  
>>>>
>>>> Jesse
>>>>
